faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in my College Point home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common ants (up to 5/8 inch long), are typically black or dark brown, and leave behind piles of wood shavings called frass. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood but excavate it to build nests. If you see large ants near wooden structures, hear rustling sounds in walls, or notice sawdust-like material, you likely have carpenter ants.

faqs.question: Why are carpenter ants common in College Point?
faqs.answer: College Point's proximity to water sources like Flushing Bay and Powell's Cove creates humid conditions that attract carpenter ants. Many homes in the area have mature trees, wooden decks, and older construction with wood-to-soil contact points, providing ideal nesting sites. Moisture issues in basements and crawl spaces common in waterfront properties also make College Point homes particularly vulnerable.
